name: Ritvo Autism Asperger Diagnostic Scale-14
description: A 14-item self-report screening instrument for autism spectrum disorder in adults, derived from the 80-item RAADS-R
instructions: Please select the response that best describes how each statement applies to you. Consider your experiences across your lifetime, not just currently.

standard.label: True only when I was young (under 16)
True now and when I was young

reversed.label: True only when I was young (under 16)
True now and when I was young

subscales.name: Mentalizing Deficits
subscales.description: Difficulties with theory of mind and understanding others' mental states

subscales.name: Social Anxiety
subscales.description: Anxiety related to social situations and interactions

severityBands.label: Below Threshold
severityBands.description: Score below the clinical cutoff; ASD unlikely based on this screening

severityBands.label: Above Threshold - Mild Symptoms
severityBands.description: Score above cutoff suggesting possible ASD; further evaluation recommended

severityBands.label: Above Threshold - Moderate Symptoms
severityBands.description: Score indicates moderate autistic traits; comprehensive ASD evaluation recommended

severityBands.label: Above Threshold - High Symptoms
severityBands.description: Score indicates significant autistic traits strongly suggestive of ASD; comprehensive evaluation strongly recommended

riskRules.message: RAADS-14 score ≥ 14 indicates screening positive for possible autism spectrum disorder; referral for comprehensive evaluation recommended
Elevated mentalizing deficits subscale score suggests significant difficulties with theory of mind
Elevated sensory reactivity subscale score suggests significant sensory processing differences

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The RAADS-14 is a screening instrument only and cannot diagnose ASD. A score of 14 or above has been validated as an appropriate cutoff for further evaluation. The scale has high sensitivity (97%) and specificity (46%) for ASD in clinical populations. All positive screens should be followed up with comprehensive diagnostic evaluation.

cutoffExplanation.description: A cutoff score of ≥14 maximizes sensitivity to identify individuals who may have ASD while accepting lower specificity (higher false positive rate). This is appropriate for a screening instrument where missing true cases is more costly than over-referral.

subscaleInterpretation.mentalizing: Higher scores indicate greater difficulty understanding others' thoughts, feelings, and intentions (theory of mind deficits)
subscaleInterpretation.social_anxiety: Higher scores indicate greater anxiety and difficulty in social situations
subscaleInterpretation.sensory_reactivity: Higher scores indicate greater sensory sensitivity and atypical sensory experiences

actions.below_threshold: No immediate action required based on screening. However, if clinical suspicion remains, consider full RAADS-R or comprehensive evaluation
actions.above_threshold_mild: Recommend referral for comprehensive ASD evaluation. Discuss findings with patient and explore their experiences
actions.above_threshold_moderate: Recommend comprehensive ASD evaluation. Consider referral to specialist (psychologist, psychiatrist, or neurologist with ASD expertise)
actions.above_threshold_high: Strongly recommend comprehensive ASD evaluation by specialist. High likelihood of ASD diagnosis; prioritize referral

limitations: Self-report measure may be affected by insight and self-awareness
Not validated for individuals with intellectual disability
Cannot differentiate ASD from other conditions with overlapping features
Lower specificity means many false positives; positive screen requires follow-up
references: Eriksson JM, Andersen LM, Bejerot S. RAADS-14 Screen: validity of a screening tool for autism spectrum disorder in an adult psychiatric population. Mol Autism. 2013;4(1):49.
Ritvo RA, Ritvo ER, Guthrie D, et al. The Ritvo Autism Asperger Diagnostic Scale-Revised (RAADS-R). J Autism Dev Disord. 2011;41(8):1076-1089.
